methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Rainbow Robotics, a robotics and manufacturing company founded by Oh Jun-ho, was acquired after raising $50M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 12 same-sector peers: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient filtering. While 12 robotics and manufacturing peers exist in the dataset, none met the narrow stage-matching criteria (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers required minimum 3 datapoints). The public comparables set consists of a single Korean-listed entity, and the private peer set, though substantive at eight firms, lacks sufficient same-stage clustering to support a credible median-based valuation multiple.

Given the data constraints, no triangulated valuation range can be reliably established from the available methods. The fundamental uncertainty stems from stage and vintage mismatch within the peer set rather than peer scarcity at the sector level. A more complete valuation would require either: expanded stage-adjacent peer identification within robotics; explicit transaction multiples from the acquisition itself; or internal financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, growth rates) for both target and comparables. Without these inputs, quantitative synthesis remains unsupported by the current dataset.
